data.title: Monad Cuts Block Time to 300 Milliseconds Under MIP-12 Proposal
data.description: Monad announced that, under the MIP-12 proposal, it will reduce block time from 400 milliseconds to 300 milliseconds today at 10:30 AM ET on July 23. According to Foresight News, the change will take effect at the scheduled time.

data.title: Uniswap Launches Permissioned Pools Framework for Tokenized Funds and Stocks
data.description: Uniswap has launched a Permissioned Pools framework for tokenized funds, stocks, and other regulated assets. According to Odaily, the feature allows tokenized asset issuers to enforce investor eligibility requirements directly on-chain while using Uniswap’s automated trading infrastructure.

data.title: Uniswap Labs Launches Permissioned Pools for Regulated Assets
data.description:
Uniswap Labs is rolling out Permissioned Pools, a framework designed for tokenized funds, equities and other regulated assets. According to NS3.AI, the feature allows issuers to limit trading to approved investors while still using Uniswap’s automated market maker.

Launch partners include Securitize, Superstate and Dowgo. The rollout adds a permissioned structure to Uniswap’s trading framework for regulated assets.

data.title: TownSquare Sonar Public Sale Exceeds 91 Million Dollars, TOWN Pre-TGE Valuation Stays Above 250 Million Dollars
data.description:
TownSquare's Sonar public sale has been oversubscribed, and the premarket price is 25% above the public sale price. According to Odaily, as of the time of writing, the public sale on Sonar had raised more than 91 million dollars, about 10% above the target.

The TOWN token asset's pre-TGE premarket price on Aspecta also remained above a 250 million dollar valuation. According to Odaily, as one of the few Sonar public sale projects listed on Polymarket prediction markets, the probability of the public sale exceeding 1 million dollars is 99%, the probability of exceeding 1.25 million dollars is 73%, and the probability of exceeding 1.5 million dollars is 52%.

data.title: Anthropic Considers Tighter Employee Stock Sale Windows
data.description: Anthropic is considering requiring ordinary employees to sell shares only during strict trading windows to avoid potential violations of insider trading laws, according to Jiemian News. The proposal was reported on July 23, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: vivo Hands Control of India Manufacturing to Dixon in JV Deal
data.description:
vivo’s India unit has received approval from India’s Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade for a restructuring that will place its 12-year manufacturing operation into a joint venture controlled 51% by local partner Dixon Technologies, according to Jiemian News. Dixon disclosed the approval to the stock exchange on July 9, after the authorization letter was issued on July 8.

The new company has paid-in capital of only 50 million rupees, but it will take over a factory with planned annual capacity of more than 100 million phones and more than 10,000 workers. The deal also covers part of vivo’s manufacturing assets, a manufacturing and packaging agreement with vivo India, and orders for about two-thirds of vivo’s India smartphone sales, or more than 20 million units based on 2025 volumes.

vivo’s India business was the company’s largest overseas market in 2025, with 32.1 million shipments and a 21% share, while its global overseas revenue already exceeds 50% and is targeted to reach 60% this year and 70% in 2027. Jiemian News reported that the Indian manufacturing exit follows years of regulatory pressure, including India’s Press Note 3 policy and tax and customs scrutiny of Chinese handset makers.

data.title: US Bitcoin ETFs Record 1,064 BTC Net Inflow, Ethereum ETFs Add 37,753 ETH
data.description: U.S. Bitcoin ETFs recorded a net inflow of 1,064 BTC today, while their 7-day net inflow reached 10,891 BTC. According to Odaily, U.S. Ethereum ETFs posted a net inflow of 37,753 ETH today, and their 7-day net inflow totaled 84,364 ETH.

data.title: Ondo Finance Subsidiary Wins FINRA Approval for Tokenized Stocks and Funds
data.description:
Foresight News reported that Ondo Finance's SEC-registered broker-dealer subsidiary, Oasis Pro Markets, has received FINRA authorization to offer compliant tokenized company stocks and funds to U.S. financial institutions and retail investors under SEC and FINRA oversight. According to Foresight News, the authorization covers OTC retail trading, underwritten primary offerings, and private placements, supporting primary issuance of tokenized securities by U.S. issuers and secondary trading by institutional and retail investors.

The tradable assets include NMS stocks, ETFs, mutual funds, index funds, and IPO securities. Settlement can be completed in fiat or supported stablecoins. The move builds on Ondo's acquisition of Oasis Pro, which holds SEC-registered broker-dealer, ATS, and transfer agent licenses, and supports a unified account structure that allows institutions, registered investment advisers, and retirement accounts to access the market through existing brokerage channels with lower onboarding friction.

data.title: Brazil Races to Safeguard $1.8 Billion in Meat Exports to EU
data.description: Brazil is rushing to avert a ban on animal-product shipments to the European Union, with more than $1.8 billion of trade from the world’s top beef and poultry exporter at risk, according to Bloomberg. The potential ban threatens a key export stream for Brazil.

data.title: LITEB Reaching a New All-Time High, Increase of 6.93% in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 23, 2026, 13:46 PM(UTC). according to Binance Market Data, LITEB has achieved a new all-time high, trading at 888.52 USDT. The 24-hour increase of 6.93%

data.title: Address Opens $4.945 Million Long on ChangXin Memory Technologies and $4.04 Million Long on SK Hynix
data.description: An address opened a $4.945 million long position in ChangXin Memory Technologies on Hyperliquid. According to NS3.AI, the same address also opened a $4.04 million long position in SK Hynix. The moves show the address taking bullish positions in two memory chip-related names on the platform.

data.title: Eli Lilly plans 2027 filing for next-gen obesity drug
data.description: According to CNBC, Eli Lilly plans to file for approval of its next-generation obesity drug in 2027 and said injectable retatrutide cleared two late-stage trials with substantial weight loss and improved glycemic control. Lilly also said it needs more time to verify manufacturing and quality-control data before filing with regulators.

data.title: STOCKS | American Airlines Falls More Than 7% to Six-Week Low After Q3 Loss Outlook
data.description: American Airlines fell more than 7% to a six-week low after the company said it expects a third-quarter loss, while the market had expected a profit. According to Jin10, American Airlines (AAL.O) dropped on the outlook.

data.title: STOCKS | Thermo Fisher Scientific Rises 9.9% After Second-Quarter Profit Tops Expectations
data.description: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO.N) rose 9.9% after second-quarter profit beat expectations. According to Jin10, the move came after the company reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter profit.

data.title: STOCKS | XJ Intelligent Foresees Smaller First-Half Loss
data.description:
XJ Intelligent (6106) expects a net loss of no more than RMB 40.5 million for the first half, down at least 20% from the corresponding period in 2025, and an adjusted net loss of no more than RMB 13.2 million, down at least 40%, according to Ming Pao.

The company said the improvement was mainly driven by higher gross profit from revenue growth, as well as gradually improving operating efficiency and cost control.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon Supports Clarity Act for Digital Asset Regulation
data.description:
Goldman Sachs Chairman and Chief Executive David Solomon publicly backed the Clarity Act, which seeks to create a new regulatory framework for the digital asset market. According to Foresight News, Solomon said in an interview that the bill is not perfect, but its importance lies in creating a level playing field, improving market stability, and allowing these markets to develop properly. He added that he strongly supports moving the bill forward.

The comments came as Republican senators began circulating a new draft of the bill and planned a full Senate vote next week. The stance contrasts with opposition from other bankers, including JPMorgan Chase Chief Executive Jamie Dimon. Critics say the bill could create an unfair advantage for crypto companies that offer stablecoin yield rewards, potentially posing risks to consumer banks. The American Bankers Association, the Bank Policy Institute, and other industry groups have also said the bill's stablecoin provisions threaten local credit that supports U.S. economic activity.

The legislation also includes ethics provisions that would prohibit federal officials from issuing their own tokens. Democratic senators said the revised text does not offer enough concessions and are expected to continue pushing for changes before the vote.

data.title: Gorae To Put 50,000 EV Chargers On-Chain In South Korea This Year
data.description:
Sui said electric vehicle charging project Gorae will bring more than 50,000 charging stations on-chain in South Korea this year through cooperation with Sui and the decentralized storage network Walrus. According to Foresight News, Gorae is working with one of South Korea's largest charging station operators to use Sui for payment settlement and Walrus to record each charging session as verifiable data in the same transaction.

Gorae plans to begin a phased rollout of South Korea's network on-chain in the second half of 2026, then expand to Asia-Pacific, Europe and North America.

data.title: Capita Economics: Gulf Recovery Faces New Setback as Iran Attacks Raise Downside Risks
data.description: According to Jin10, Capita Economics assistant economist Nicolas Crittenden said a new round of Iranian attacks could undermine the Gulf region's early economic recovery. He added that the latest escalation could reverse the improvement seen after last month's U.S.-Iran ceasefire eased disruptions to regional trade and energy supplies. Crittenden said private-sector activity across the Gulf improved in June, led by Saudi Arabia, while exports to the region from major trading partners also increased and flight numbers returned to near pre-conflict levels, signaling a recovery in tourism and logistics. He said renewed disruption to traffic through the Strait of Hormuz could stall the recovery, and downside risks are building around Capita Economics' forecast that Gulf GDP will shrink by about 6% this year.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple Maps to Be Integrated Into Ford Vehicles' UEV Platform in 2027
data.description: According to Jin10, Apple Maps will be installed on Ford vehicles' UEV electronic platform in 2027.

data.title: DeFi Tokenized RWA Use Returns to About $3.77 Billion After KelpDAO Exploit
data.description: Active use of tokenized real-world assets in DeFi has returned to about $3.77 billion, nearly reaching the level seen before the April 18 KelpDAO exploit. According to NS3.AI, DefiLlama tracking shows the recovery took roughly 95 days from the shock to July 22. The exploit released about $292 million in unbacked rsETH and triggered a $13 billion decline across DeFi in 48 hours.
